revision.text:
'''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]] == 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認され、[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座し、「深川のこんぴらさん」として親しまれていた。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、御神霊は讃岐本宮に遷された。
[[1956年]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、[[讃岐国|讃岐]]高松藩[[下屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793年]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせ祀ることとなり、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）遷座再興した。 == 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日 == 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 : 東京都文京区本郷一丁目5番11号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[水道橋駅]]（[[中央・総武緩行線|JR中央線]]下車徒歩5分・[[都営地下鉄三田線]]下車徒歩3分） == 関連項目 == * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[金刀比羅神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Osm box|w|576806820}} * [http://www.konpira.or.jp/about/branch/tokyo.html 金刀比羅宮公式サイト] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらくうとうきようふんしや}} [[Category:文京区の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:本郷]]

revision.comment: /* 由緒 */ wikipediaでは改行記号は使用しないため
revision.text:
'''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]
== 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認され、[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座し、「深川のこんぴらさん」として親しまれていた。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、御神霊は讃岐本宮に遷された。
[[1956年]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、[[讃岐国|讃岐]]高松藩[[下屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793年]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日
== 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 : 東京都文京区本郷一丁目5番11号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[水道橋駅]]（[[中央・総武緩行線|JR中央線]]下車徒歩5分・[[都営地下鉄三田線]]下車徒歩3分）
== 関連項目 == <!-- 関連するウィキリンク、ウィキ間リンク --> * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[金刀比羅神社]]
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|w|576806820}} * [http://www.konpira.or.jp/about/branch/tokyo.html 金刀比羅宮公式サイト]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらくうとうきようふんしや}} [[Category:文京区の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:本郷]]

'''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]] == 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮東京分社 |画像 = [[File:FNic7yqaIAYd4RS.png|240px]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[文京区]][[本郷 (文京区)|本郷]]1-5-11 |地図 = |祭神 = [[大物主|大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]] |社格 = |創建 = [[文政]]2年（[[1819年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月20日 |神事 = }} '''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]
== 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。
[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、戦後御神霊は[[讃岐国|讃岐]]本宮の旭社に遷されていた。
[[1956年]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、讃岐高松藩[[上屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793年]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地（[[下屋敷]]跡）を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日

== 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。
[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、戦後御神霊は[[讃岐国|讃岐]]本宮の旭社に遷されていた。

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮東京分社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:FNic7yqaIAYd4RS.png|240px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[東京都]][[文京区]][[本郷 (文京区)|本郷]]1-5-11 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E|display=inline,title}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = [[大物主|大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = [[文政]]2年（[[1819年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 5月20日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=13.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=45|lon_sec=21.5|label=金刀比羅宮東京分社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E}}}} }} '''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]
== 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。
[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、戦後御神霊は[[讃岐国|讃岐]]本宮の旭社に遷されていた。
[[1956年]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、讃岐高松藩[[上屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793年]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地（[[下屋敷]]跡）を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日
== 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 : 東京都文京区本郷一丁目5番11号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[水道橋駅]]（[[中央・総武緩行線|JR中央線]]下車徒歩5分・[[都営地下鉄三田線]]下車徒歩3分）
== 関連項目 == <!-- 関連するウィキリンク、ウィキ間リンク --> * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[金刀比羅神社]]
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|w|576806820}} * [http://www.konpira.or.jp/about/branch/tokyo.html 金刀比羅宮公式サイト]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[金刀比羅宮東京分社]] to [[Kotohira-gū Tokyo Branch]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮東京分社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:FNic7yqaIAYd4RS.png|240px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[東京都]][[文京区]][[本郷 (文京区)|本郷]]1-5-11 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E|display=inline,title}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = [[大物主|大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = [[文政]]2年（[[1819年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 5月20日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=13.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=45|lon_sec=21.5|label=金刀比羅宮東京分社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E}}}} }} '''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]
== 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。
[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、戦後御神霊は[[讃岐国|讃岐]]本宮の旭社に遷されていた。
[[1956年]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、讃岐高松藩[[上屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793年]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地（[[下屋敷]]跡）を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日
== 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 : 東京都文京区本郷一丁目5番11号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[水道橋駅]]（[[中央・総武緩行線|JR中央線]]下車徒歩5分・[[都営地下鉄三田線]]下車徒歩3分）
== 関連項目 == <!-- 関連するウィキリンク、ウィキ間リンク --> * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[金刀比羅神社]]
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|w|576806820}} * [http://www.konpira.or.jp/about/branch/tokyo.html 金刀比羅宮公式サイト]

== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]
== 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。
[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、戦後御神霊は[[讃岐国|讃岐]]本宮の旭社に遷されていた。
[[1956年]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、讃岐高松藩[[上屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793年]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地（[[下屋敷]]跡）を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日
== 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 : 東京都文京区本郷一丁目5番11号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[水道橋駅]]（[[中央・総武緩行線|JR中央線]]下車徒歩5分・[[都営地下鉄三田線]]下車徒歩3分）

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮東京分社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:FNic7yqaIAYd4RS.png|240px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[東京都]][[文京区]][[本郷 (文京区)|本郷]]1-5-11 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E|display=inline,title}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = [[大物主|大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = [[文政]]2年（[[1819年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 5月20日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=13.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=45|lon_sec=21.5|label=金刀比羅宮東京分社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E}}}} }} '''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]
== 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。
[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、戦後御神霊は[[讃岐国|讃岐]]本宮の旭社に遷されていた。
[[1956年]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、讃岐高松藩[[上屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793年]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地（[[下屋敷]]跡）を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日

[[1956]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、讃岐高松藩[[上屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地（[[下屋敷]]跡）を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮東京分社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:FNic7yqaIAYd4RS.png|240px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[東京都]][[文京区]][[本郷 (文京区)|本郷]]1-5-11 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E|display=inline,title}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = [[大物主|大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = [[文政]]2年（[[1819]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = 5月20日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=13.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=45|lon_sec=21.5|label=金刀比羅宮東京分社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E}}}} }} '''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。
== Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]
== 由緒 == [[1819]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。
[[1888]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄のKeigai-Masshaに加えられた。
[[1890]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、戦後御神霊は[[讃岐国|讃岐]]本宮の旭社に遷されていた。
[[1956]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、讃岐高松藩[[上屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地（[[下屋敷]]跡）を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日
== 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 : 東京都文京区本郷一丁目5番11号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[水道橋駅]]（[[中央・総武緩行線|JR中央線]]下車徒歩5分・[[都営地下鉄三田線]]下車徒歩3分） == See Also == <!-- 関連するウィキリンク、ウィキ間リンク --> * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[金刀比羅神社]]
== External Links == {{Osm box|w|576806820}} * [http://www.konpira.or.jp/about/branch/tokyo.html 金刀比羅宮公式サイト]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}

{{infobox shinto | name = Kotohira Shrine Tokyo Branch | image = [[File:FNic7yqaIAYd4RS.png|240px]] | address = [[Tokyo]] [[Bunkyō]] [[Hongō (Bunkyō)|Hongō]] 1-5-11 | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} no Mikoto, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | rank = | founded = [[Bunsei]] 2 ({{ill|1819|ja|1819年}}) | main_hall = | alias = Kotohira Shrine | pilgrimage_sites = | annual_festival = May 20 | shinto_rituals = | map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=13.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=45|lon_sec=21.5|label=Kotohira Shrine Tokyo Branch|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E}}}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine Tokyo Branch''' (Kotohira-gū Tōkyō Bunsha) is a [[shrine]] located in [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] ([[Branch shrine|bunsha]] of [[Kotohira Shrine]] in [[Kotohira, Nakatado District, Kagawa]]). The shrine crest is a circle with gold.
== Deities == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} no Mikoto * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉穗魂}}
== History == In {{ill|1819|ja|1819年}} ({{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政}} 2), [[Itabashi-shuku|Kami-shuku]] headman {{ill|Itabashi Shizaemon|ja|板橋市左衛門}} enshrined {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金比羅大権現}} on his property. In {{ill|1880|ja|1880年}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 13), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mugakusha|ja|無格社}} named Kotohira Shrine.
In {{ill|1888|ja|1888年}} (Meiji 21), the shrine was moved to [[Kanda Izumi-chō]] and became a directly controlled branch of [[Kotohira Shrine]].
In {{ill|1890|ja|1890年}} (Meiji 23), it was moved to [[Fukagawa Koishikawa-chō]]. The shrine buildings were destroyed during the [[Great Kantō earthquake]] and the [[Tokyo air raids]], and the spirit was transferred to the Asahi-sha of the main shrine in [[Sanuki Province]].
In {{ill|1956|ja|1956年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 31), the site of the Kotohira Shrine at [[Suidōbashi]] (founded in {{ill|1793|ja|1793年}}) by the [[Matsudaira clan]] of the former [[Takamatsu Domain]] was donated by {{ill|Matsudaira Yoriaki|ja|松平頼明}}. The two shrines were merged, and the current site was established in {{ill|1964|ja|1964年}} (Shōwa 39).
== Annual Festivals == * January 10 - Hatsutoka-sai * February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by sumo wrestlers from {{ill|Sadogatake stable|ja|佐波ヶ崎部屋}}) * May 19-20 - Annual Festival
== Location Information == ; Address : 1-5-11 Hongō, Bunkyō-ku, Tokyo ; Transportation :* Railway :** [[Suidōbashi Station]] (5-minute walk from JR Chūō-Sōbu Line and 3-minute walk from Toei Mita Line)
== See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] * [[Kotohira Shrine]]
== External links == * [http://www.konpira.or.jp/about/branch/tokyo.html Official Kotohira Shrine Site]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}}
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Bunkyō, Tokyo]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrine]] [[Category:Hongō]] [[ja:金刀比羅宮東京分社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅宮東京分社 |画像 = [[File:FNic7yqaIAYd4RS.png|240px]] |所在地 = [[東京都]][[文京区]][[本郷 (文京区)|本郷]]1-5-11 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主|大物主命]]、[[崇徳天皇]] |社格 = |創建 = [[文政]]2年（[[1819年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月20日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|lat_deg=35|lat_min=42|lat_sec=13.5|lon_deg=139|lon_min=45|lon_sec=21.5|label=金刀比羅宮東京分社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|42|13.5|N|139|45|21.5|E}}}} }} '''金刀比羅宮東京分社'''（ことひらぐう・とうきょうぶんしゃ） は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]にある[[神社]]（[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の分社）である。神紋は丸に金。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]
== 由緒 == [[1819年]]（[[文政]]2年）、[[中山道]][[板橋宿|下板橋宿上宿]]の[[名主]]板橋市左衛門が所有地内に[[金刀比羅宮|金毘羅大権現]]を観請し、邸内祠として祀られたのがはじまり。[[1880年]]（[[明治]]13年）、[[無格社]]・金刀比羅神社として公認された。
[[1888年]]（明治21年）に[[神田和泉町]]に遷座し、[[金刀比羅宮]]直轄の境外末社に加えられた。
[[1890年]]（明治23年）に[[古石場|深川古石場町]]に遷座。[[関東大震災]]と[[東京大空襲]]ではいずれも建造物を焼失し、戦後御神霊は[[讃岐国|讃岐]]本宮の旭社に遷されていた。
[[1956年]]([[昭和]]31年)、元[[高松藩]]主[[高松松平家|松平家]]当主の[[松平頼明 (伯爵)|松平頼明]]から、讃岐高松藩[[上屋敷]]の邸内社として[[1793年]]（[[寛政]]5年）に創建された[[水道橋 (神田川)|水道橋]]金刀比羅神社の境内地（[[下屋敷]]跡）を再建用地として寄進を受けたことで二社を合わせて祀ることとなり、[[1964年]]（昭和39年）に遷座再興した。
== 年間祭典 == *1月10日 - 初十日祭 *2月3日 - 節分祭（[[佐渡ヶ嶽部屋]]の力士による豆まき） *5月19・20日 - 例祭日
== 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 : 東京都文京区本郷一丁目5番11号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[水道橋駅]]（[[中央・総武緩行線|JR中央線]]下車徒歩5分・[[都営地下鉄三田線]]下車徒歩3分）
== 関連項目 == <!-- 関連するウィキリンク、ウィキ間リンク --> * [[東京都の神社一覧]] * [[金刀比羅神社]]
== 外部リンク == {{Osm box|w|576806820}} * [http://www.konpira.or.jp/about/branch/tokyo.html 金刀比羅宮公式サイト]

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]]. It is a branch of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]] [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]]. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama deity]]

== History ==
[[:en:1819|1819]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|qq=|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from [[:en:Itabashi-shuku|Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku]], enshrined [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Great Authority]] on his property as a family shrine. In [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21 year), the shrine was relocated to [[:en:Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|Kanda Izumicho]] and added to the peripheral shrines directly under [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]].

In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|qq=|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|qq=|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|qq=|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

== Location Information ==
; Location
: [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]] Hongo 1-5-11
; Transportation
:* Railway
:** [[:en:Suidōbashi Station|Suidobashi Station]] (5-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Chūō-Sōbu Line|JR Chuo Line]] and 3-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Toei Mita Line|Toei Mita Line]])

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]]. It is a branch of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]] [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]]. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama deity]]

== History ==
[[:en:1819|1819]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|qq=|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from [[:en:Itabashi-shuku|Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku]], enshrined [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Great Authority]] on his property as a family shrine. In [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21 year), the shrine was relocated to [[:en:Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|Kanda Izumicho]] and added to the peripheral shrines directly under [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]].

In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|qq=|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|qq=|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|qq=|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|qq=|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

== Location Information ==
; Location
: [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]] Hongo 1-5-11
; Transportation
:* Railway
:** [[:en:Suidōbashi Station|Suidobashi Station]] (5-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Chūō-Sōbu Line|JR Chuo Line]] and 3-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Toei Mita Line|Toei Mita Line]])

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]]. It is a branch of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]] [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]]. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama deity]]

== History ==
[[:en:1819|1819]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from [[:en:Itabashi-shuku|Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku]], enshrined [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Great Authority]] on his property as a family shrine. In [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21 year), the shrine was relocated to [[:en:Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|Kanda Izumicho]] and added to the peripheral shrines directly under [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]].

In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]]. It is a branch of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]] [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]]. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama deity]]

== History ==
[[:en:1819|1819]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from [[:en:Itabashi-shuku|Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku]], enshrined [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Great Authority]] on his property as a family shrine. In [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21 year), the shrine was relocated to [[:en:Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|Kanda Izumicho]] and added to the peripheral shrines directly under [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]].

In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

== Location Information ==
; Location
: [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]] Hongo 1-5-11
; Transportation
:* Railway
:** [[:en:Suidōbashi Station|Suidobashi Station]] (5-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Chūō-Sōbu Line|JR Chuo Line]] and 3-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Toei Mita Line|Toei Mita Line]])

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]]. It is a branch of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]] [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]]. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama deity]]

== History ==
[[:en:1819|1819]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from [[:en:Itabashi-shuku|Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku]], enshrined [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Great Authority]] on his property as a family shrine. In [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21 year), the shrine was relocated to [[:en:Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|Kanda Izumicho]] and added to the peripheral shrines directly under [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]].

In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

== Location Information ==
; Location
: [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]] Hongo 1-5-11
; Transportation
:* Railway
:** [[:en:Suidōbashi Station|Suidobashi Station]] (5-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Chūō-Sōbu Line|JR Chuo Line]] and 3-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Toei Mita Line|Toei Mita Line]])

In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]]. It is a branch of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]] [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]]. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama deity]]

== History ==
[[:en:1819|1819]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from [[:en:Itabashi-shuku|Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku]], enshrined [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Great Authority]] on his property as a family shrine. In [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21 year), the shrine was relocated to [[:en:Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|Kanda Izumicho]] and added to the peripheral shrines directly under [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]].

In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]]. It is a branch of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]] [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]]. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama deity]]

== History ==
[[:en:1819|1819]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from [[:en:Itabashi-shuku|Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku]], enshrined [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Great Authority]] on his property as a family shrine. In [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21 year), the shrine was relocated to [[:en:Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|Kanda Izumicho]] and added to the peripheral shrines directly under [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]].

In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].

In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

== Location Information ==
; Location
: [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]] Hongo 1-5-11
; Transportation
:* Railway
:** [[:en:Suidōbashi Station|Suidobashi Station]] (5-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Chūō-Sōbu Line|JR Chuo Line]] and 3-minute walk from alighting at [[:en:Toei Mita Line|Toei Mita Line]])

This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]])
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

; Location
: [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]] Hongo 1-5-11

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]]. It is a branch of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]] located in [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Nakatado District, Kagawa|Nakatado District]] [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira]]. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.
== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama deity]]
== History == [[:en:1819|1819]] ([[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from [[:en:Itabashi-shuku|Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku]], enshrined [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Great Authority]] on his property as a family shrine. In [[:en:1880|1880]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21 year), the shrine was relocated to [[:en:Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|Kanda Izumicho]] and added to the peripheral shrines directly under [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Konpira Shrine]].
In [[:en:1890|1890]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki]].
In [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in [[:en:1793|1793]] ([[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in [[:en:1964|1964]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]] 39 year).
== Annual Festival == * January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival * February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from [[:en:Sadogatake stable|Sado Gakatake stable]]) * May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo}}. It is a branch of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine}} located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Nakatado District, Kagawa|en|Nakatado District, Kagawa|WD=Q1206155|lt=Nakatado District}} {{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|en|Kotohira, Kagawa|WD=Q1271483|lt=Kotohira}}. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama deity}}

== History ==
{{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei era}} 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from {{ill|Itabashi-shuku|en|Itabashi-shuku|WD=Q3155832|lt=Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku}}, enshrined {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Great Authority}} on his property as a family shrine. In {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 21 year), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q3347167|lt=Kanda Izumicho}} and added to the peripheral shrines directly under {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine}}.

In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Tokyo air raid}} burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki}}.

In {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu Domain}}, belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in {{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei era}} 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from {{ill|Sadogatake stable|en|Sadogatake stable|WD=Q7398024|lt=Sado Gakatake stable}})
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

== Location Information ==
; Location

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo}}. It is a branch of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine}} located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Nakatado District, Kagawa|en|Nakatado District, Kagawa|WD=Q1206155|lt=Nakatado District}} {{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|en|Kotohira, Kagawa|WD=Q1271483|lt=Kotohira}}. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama deity}}

== History ==
{{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei era}} 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from {{ill|Itabashi-shuku|en|Itabashi-shuku|WD=Q3155832|lt=Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku}}, enshrined {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Great Authority}} on his property as a family shrine. In {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 21 year), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q3347167|lt=Kanda Izumicho}} and added to the peripheral shrines directly under {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine}}.

In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Tokyo air raid}} burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki}}.

In {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu Domain}}, belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in {{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei era}} 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from {{ill|Sadogatake stable|en|Sadogatake stable|WD=Q7398024|lt=Sado Gakatake stable}})
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

== Location Information ==
; Location
: {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo}} Hongo 1-5-11
; Transportation
:* Railway
:** {{ill|Suidōbashi Station|en|Suidōbashi Station|WD=Q801496|lt=Suidobashi Station}} (5-minute walk from alighting at {{ill|Chūō-Sōbu Line|en|Chūō-Sōbu Line|WD=Q1091295|lt=JR Chuo Line}} and 3-minute walk from alighting at {{ill|Toei Mita Line|en|Toei Mita Line|WD=Q1374506|lt=Toei Mita Line}})

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo}}. It is a branch of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine}} located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Nakatado District, Kagawa|en|Nakatado District, Kagawa|WD=Q1206155|lt=Nakatado District}} {{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|en|Kotohira, Kagawa|WD=Q1271483|lt=Kotohira}}. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== {{nihongo|Worshipped deities|祭神|Saijin}} ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|lt=Ōmononushi deity|lt_ja=大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama deity}}

== History ==
{{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei era}} 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from {{ill|Itabashi-shuku|en|Itabashi-shuku|WD=Q3155832|lt=Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku}}, enshrined {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Great Authority}} on his property as a family shrine. In {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 21 year), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q3347167|lt=Kanda Izumicho}} and added to the peripheral shrines directly under {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine}}.

In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Tokyo air raid}} burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki}}.

In {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu Domain}}, belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in {{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei era}} 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from {{ill|Sadogatake stable|en|Sadogatake stable|WD=Q7398024|lt=Sado Gakatake stable}})
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

== Location Information ==
; Location
: {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo}} Hongo 1-5-11
; Transportation
:* Railway
:** {{ill|Suidōbashi Station|en|Suidōbashi Station|WD=Q801496|lt=Suidobashi Station}} (5-minute walk from alighting at {{ill|Chūō-Sōbu Line|en|Chūō-Sōbu Line|WD=Q1091295|lt=JR Chuo Line}} and 3-minute walk from alighting at {{ill|Toei Mita Line|en|Toei Mita Line|WD=Q1374506|lt=Toei Mita Line}})

== History ==
{{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei era}} 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from {{ill|Itabashi-shuku|en|Itabashi-shuku|WD=Q3155832|lt=Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku}}, enshrined {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Great Authority}} on his property as a family shrine. In {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked Shrine|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 21 year), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q3347167|lt=Kanda Izumicho}} and added to the peripheral shrines directly under {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine}}.

In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Tokyo air raid}} burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki}}.

In {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu Domain}}, belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in {{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei era}} 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}} 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from {{ill|Sadogatake stable|en|Sadogatake stable|WD=Q7398024|lt=Sado Gakatake stable}})
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

: {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo}} Hongo 1-5-11
; Transportation

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo|1=Bunkyō}}. It is a branch of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}} located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Nakatado District, Kagawa|en|Nakatado District, Kagawa|WD=Q1206155|lt=Nakatado District|1=Nakatado District, Kagawa}} {{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|en|Kotohira, Kagawa|WD=Q1271483|lt=Kotohira|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888|1=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 21 year), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q3347167|lt=Kanda Izumicho|1=Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo}} and added to the peripheral shrines directly under {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}}.

In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Tokyo air raid|1=Bombing of Tokyo}} burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki|1=Sanuki Province}}.

In {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956|1=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu Domain|1=Takamatsu Domain}}, belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in {{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793|1=1793}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei era|1=Kansei}} 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 39 year).
