== History ==
{{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819|1=1819}} ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei era|1=Bunsei}} 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from {{ill|Itabashi-shuku|en|Itabashi-shuku|WD=Q3155832|lt=Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku|1=Itabashi-shuku}}, enshrined {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Great Authority|1=Kotohira-gū}} on his property as a family shrine. In {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|WD=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888|1=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 21 year), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|WD=Q3347167|lt=Kanda Izumicho|1=Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo}} and added to the peripheral shrines directly under {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}}.

In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} and the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|WD=Q59886|lt=Tokyo air raid|1=Bombing of Tokyo}} burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki|1=Sanuki Province}}.

In {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956|1=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|WD=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu Domain|1=Takamatsu Domain}}, belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in {{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793|1=1793}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei era|1=Kansei}} 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in {{ill|1964|en|1964|WD=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from {{ill|Sadogatake stable|en|Sadogatake stable|WD=Q7398024|lt=Sado Gakatake stable|1=Sadogatake stable}})
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days

'''Konpira-gu Tokyo Bunsha''' (金刀比羅宮東京分社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo|1=Bunkyō}}. It is a branch of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}} located in {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Nakatado District, Kagawa|en|Nakatado District, Kagawa|WD=Q1206155|lt=Nakatado District|1=Nakatado District, Kagawa}} {{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|en|Kotohira, Kagawa|WD=Q1271483|lt=Kotohira|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}. The shrine's emblem is a circle with gold.

== History ==
{{ill|1819|en|1819|qid=Q6967|lt=1819|1=1819}} ({{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei era|1=Bunsei}} 2 year), a local head ({{ill|Nagushi|ja|名主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagushi|1=myōshu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110095935}}) named Itabashi Shizaemon from {{ill|Itabashi-shuku|en|Itabashi-shuku|qid=Q3155832|lt=Shimoitabashi-shuku Kamijuku|1=Itabashi-shuku}}, enshrined {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Konpira Great Authority|1=Kotohira-gū}} on his property as a family shrine. In {{ill|1880|en|1880|qid=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 13 year), it was officially recognized as a {{ill|Mukaku-sha|ja|無格社|1=Unranked shrines|lt=Hokora|qid=Q134917285}}{{efn|Hokora|lt=Unranked Shrine|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaku-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} and {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.

In {{ill|1888|en|1888|qid=Q7829|lt=1888|1=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 21 year), the shrine was relocated to {{ill|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo|qid=Q3347167|lt=Kanda Izumicho|1=Izumichō, Chiyoda, Tokyo}} and added to the peripheral shrines directly under {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Konpira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}}.

In {{ill|1890|en|1890|qid=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 23 year), it was moved to {{ill|Furuishiba|ja|古石場|lt=Fukagawa Furuishibacho|lt_ja=深川古石場町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuishiba|1=Furuishiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11411550}}. Both the {{ill|Great Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3742341}} and the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|qid=Q59886|lt=Tokyo air raid|1=Bombing of Tokyo}} burned the structures, and after the war, the sacred spirit was transferred to the Asahi Shrine of the main shrine in {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki|1=Sanuki Province}}.

In {{ill|1956|en|1956|qid=Q5221|lt=1956|1=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 31 year), from Matsudaira Yoriaki (Count) of the former {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|en|Takamatsu Domain|qid=Q1036915|lt=Takamatsu Domain|1=Takamatsu Domain}}, belonging to the {{ill|Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|ja|高松松平家|lt=Tatsudaira family|lt_ja=松平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Matsudaira Family|1=Takamatsu-Matsudaira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17219211}}, a donation of the land of the former {{ill|Kamiyashiki|ja|上屋敷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyashiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武家屋敷|1=buke yashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545899}} of the Sanuki Takamatsu Domain was received. This land, previously used for establishing the {{ill|Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|ja|水道橋 (神田川)|lt=Suidobashi|lt_ja=水道橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suidobashi (Kandagawa)|1=Suidō Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20045068}} {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, founded in {{ill|1793|en|1793|qid=Q7807|lt=1793|1=1793}} ({{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei era|1=Kansei}} 5 year), enabled the two shrines to be enshrined together, and the relocation and restoration were completed in {{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}} 39 year).

== Annual Festival ==
* January 10 - Hatsu-jūnichi Festival
* February 3 - Setsubun Festival (bean throwing by wrestlers from {{ill|Sadogatake stable|en|Sadogatake stable|qid=Q7398024|lt=Sado Gakatake stable|1=Sadogatake stable}})
* May 19–20 - Annual Festival Days
